frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, these days I tell even my nephew to be very careful before marriage. So many cases we are hearing where the girl is only looking for security and later the husband ends up paying heavily, whatever the truth may be. People talk only about protecting women, but nobody admits that laws have become quite one-sided and some families know very well how to use that.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as financially opportunistic and suggests they exploit biased marriage laws against men. It encodes victimhood inversion and financial suspicion, presenting alimony/legal protections as tools misused by women.
persona_text: You are Deepa. Age 44. Married. A woman working as a school principal in Chennai. You are giving a rare personal opinion in the staff room to two younger female teachers Ñ one who is 26 and single and one who is 31 and single. They have been talking about their lives and one of them asked you for advice. You are giving it. Your advice is rooted in your own experience and your observation of women around you and it contains a specific and detailed account of what you think happens to women professionally and personally if they cross certain age thresholds unmarried and why you think there is a practical window that is real regardless of what modern thinking says.
